profile.name: Jordan Lee
profile.headline: Customer Operations and Service Improvement Manager

source_cv.summary: Customer operations manager with nine years in service delivery, complaint resolution, team leadership and continuous improvement.

bullets: Managed 45 people across customer service, quality and workforce coordination.
Redesigned complaint triage and ownership, reducing average resolution time by 28% over nine months.
Introduced service metrics, weekly performance huddles and issue-escalation routines.
Mapped customer journeys and coordinated improvement work across service and claims teams.
Facilitated frontline workshops and translated recurring issues into workflow changes.

education: Bachelor of Business, River City University

evidence.safe_wording: Managed a 45-person customer operations function across service and quality teams.
evidence.source_excerpt: Managed 45 people across customer service, quality and workforce coordination.
restrictions: Do not increase team size or claim national accountability.

evidence.claim: Reduced complaint resolution time by 28 percent.
evidence.safe_wording: Reduced complaint resolution time by 28% through workflow redesign and service ownership changes.
evidence.source_excerpt: Redesigned complaint triage and ownership, reducing average resolution time by 28% over nine months.
restrictions: Retain the nine-month timeframe when useful.

evidence.claim: Implemented service metrics and weekly performance routines.
evidence.safe_wording: Implemented service metrics and weekly performance routines for customer operations.
evidence.source_excerpt: Introduced service metrics, weekly performance huddles and issue escalation routines.
restrictions: Do not describe as enterprise analytics ownership.

evidence.source_excerpt: Bachelor of Business, River City University.

expected_journey: Assess a supplied fixed-term role, make duration and annualised salary explicit, and avoid overstating analytics depth.
